w20: Cost-efficient micro instance for minimal workloads, featuring a shared x86_64 CPU and 1GB RAM, suitable for basic, non-demanding tasks.
c150: Google Cloud Platform f1-micro: An x86_64 instance with 1 shared vCPU and 1GB RAM, designed for ultra-low-cost, minimal computing requirements. Ideal for basic tasks.
c200: Explore Google Cloud Platform's f1-micro, a cost-effective x86_64 instance offering 1 shared vCPU and 1GB RAM. Optimized for development, testing, and very light, non-critical applications requiring minimal resources.
w150: Google Cloud Platform's f1-micro is a general-purpose micro instance, designed for minimal resource consumption. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon CPU with one shared virtual core and one physical core, supporting AVX512f, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets. The server is provisioned with 1 GB of memory. There is no bundled GPU or local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate poor results across various metrics, including single-core CPU (384.79 stress-ng score), multi-core CPU (384.79 stress-ng score), and memory bandwidth (80.8 GB/sec read-only small block, 0.2 GB/sec write large block). Passmark CPU score is 236.08, and Redis SET operations achieve 0.24 million operations/sec, both categorized in the bottom 10%. Static web serving throughput is 0.22 GB/sec. This instance is suitable for very low-demand workloads such as small development environments, basic testing, or infrequent, non-critical background tasks.
